title: Eliminate Plaintext Secrets in Helm Values and Secure Secret Injection
description: Refactor the Helm chart and deployment process to ensure that no secrets (MinIO, Redis, CNPG credentials) are ever stored in plaintext in values files or committed to version control. Implement secure secret injection at deploy time using Helm's --set/--set-file flags or CI/CD pipeline variables. Provide .gitignore rules and example templates for secret values. Consider interim use of Sealed Secrets or SOPS-encrypted values until ESO migration.
subtasks: Update .gitignore to exclude all values files containing secrets (e.g., values-staging.yaml, values-production.yaml, values-secrets.yaml).
Create a values-secrets.yaml.example template with placeholder values for all required secrets.
Refactor Helm chart documentation and README to instruct operators to inject secrets at deploy time using --set/--set-file or CI/CD variables.
Remove all hardcoded secret values from version-controlled values files.
Optionally, implement Sealed Secrets or SOPS-encrypted values as a stopgap, with documentation for their use.
Verify that no secrets are present in any committed files or ConfigMaps.
Test deployment via CI/CD pipeline with secrets injected securely at runtime.
Run a repository scan (e.g., git-secrets, truffleHog) to confirm no secrets are present in version control.
acceptance_criteria: No secrets are present in any committed values files or ConfigMaps.
values-secrets.yaml.example exists with only placeholder values.
Deployment instructions in README require secret injection at deploy time.
CI/CD pipeline injects secrets securely and no plaintext secrets are written to disk.
Repository scan confirms no secrets in version control.
Helm deployment succeeds with secrets injected at runtime.
Helm test pods pass with injected secrets.

title: Enable TLS Encryption for All Database and Message Broker Connections
description: Configure TLS for all inter-service connections to Postgres (CNPG), Redis, and NATS, as well as for ingress and inter-cluster communication. Update Helm values, backing service charts, and connection strings to use secure protocols. For v1, prioritize Postgres TLS; add Redis and NATS TLS as a fast-follow. Ensure all externally-exposed endpoints use TLS as soon as base infra is stable.
subtasks: Enable TLS in CNPG Postgres by updating spec.postgresql.pg_hba to require SSL and configuring server certificates.
Update Postgres connection strings to use sslmode=require.
Enable Redis TLS in the bitnami/redis chart (redis.tls.enabled=true) and update connection strings to use rediss://.
Enable NATS TLS (nats.config.tls) and update connection strings to use nats+tls://.
Update all Secret and ConfigMap templates to reflect TLS-enabled endpoints.
Ensure all ingress controllers and exposed services use TLS certificates (via cert-manager or self-signed as interim).
Document TLS configuration and certificate management in the README.
Test all connections with TLS enabled using Helm test pods.
Run OWASP ZAP or similar scan against all externally-exposed endpoints to verify TLS enforcement.
acceptance_criteria: All Postgres, Redis, and NATS connections are encrypted with TLS.
Connection strings in Secrets/ConfigMaps use TLS variants (sslmode=require, rediss://, nats+tls://).
Ingress controllers terminate TLS for all external endpoints.
Helm test pods succeed with TLS-enabled connections.
No plaintext protocols are used for sensitive connections.
OWASP ZAP or similar scan confirms TLS is enforced on all endpoints.

title: Document and Implement Manual Secret Rotation Procedures
description: Establish and document a manual secret rotation process for all credentials (MinIO, Redis, Postgres). Provide a runbook in the README with step-by-step instructions for generating new credentials, updating secrets, rolling restarts, and revoking old credentials. Set up a calendar reminder for quarterly rotation and track ESO migration as a follow-up.
subtasks: Write a detailed secret rotation runbook in the README covering MinIO, Redis, and Postgres credentials.
Include steps for generating new credentials, updating Helm values or Kubernetes Secrets, and rolling restarts of dependent workloads.
Document the process for revoking old credentials and verifying successful rotation.
Add a calendar reminder or ticket for quarterly secret rotation.
Reference the deferred ESO migration and target date in documentation.
Test the rotation process in a staging environment and verify all services continue to function.
acceptance_criteria: README contains a clear, step-by-step secret rotation runbook.
All credential types (MinIO, Redis, Postgres) are covered.
Rotation process is tested and validated in staging.
Calendar reminder or ticket for quarterly rotation is in place.
ESO migration is tracked as a follow-up item.

title: Restrict MinIO Bucket Setup Job to Least-Privilege and Prevent Root Credential Exposure
description: Refactor the MinIO bucket setup Job to use a dedicated MinIO admin user with only the permissions required for bucket and user management, rather than root credentials. Ensure the Job's service account has minimal RBAC, logs are scrubbed of credentials, and completed Job pods are auto-cleaned. Document the creation and protection of the root Secret as a prerequisite.
subtasks: Create a dedicated MinIO admin user with scoped permissions for bucket and user management.
Update the bucket setup Job to use the dedicated admin user's credentials instead of root.
Restrict the Job's serviceAccountName to minimal RBAC permissions.
Add log scrubbing to the Job script to prevent credential leakage.
Set ttlSecondsAfterFinished on the Job to auto-delete completed pods.
Document the process for creating and protecting the root Secret as a prerequisite in the README.
Test the Job to ensure it functions with the new admin user and does not leak credentials.
acceptance_criteria: MinIO bucket setup Job uses a dedicated admin user, not root.
Job's service account has minimal RBAC.
No credentials are present in Job logs.
Completed Job pods are auto-deleted.
Root Secret creation and protection is documented.
Job completes successfully and is idempotent.

title: Pin All Container Images to Immutable Tags or Digests
description: Update all container image references in Helm test pods and Jobs to use immutable tags (specific patch versions) or SHA256 digests. Set imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent. Optionally, configure use of a private registry mirror for image caching and scanning.
subtasks: Update all image references (e.g., postgres, redis, nats-box, minio/mc, busybox) to use specific patch versions or SHA256 digests.
Set imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent for all test pods and Jobs.
Document the image pinning policy in the README.
Optionally, configure a private registry mirror and document its use.
Test all Helm test pods and Jobs to ensure they pull the correct images and function as expected.
acceptance_criteria: All image references use immutable tags or digests.
imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent is set for all test pods and Jobs.
README documents the image pinning policy.
Helm test pods and Jobs run successfully with pinned images.

title: Apply Pod Security Standards and Harden Pod Security Contexts
description: Enforce Kubernetes Pod Security Standards (PSS) at the namespace level and set secure pod securityContext defaults for all workloads, test pods, and Jobs. Ensure pods run as non-root, with read-only root filesystems and no privilege escalation.
subtasks: Apply PodSecurityAdmission labels (e.g., pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce: restricted) to all namespaces.

Update all pod specs (including subcharts and test pods) to set securityContext: runAsNonRoot: true, readOnlyRootFilesystem: true (where possible), allowPrivilegeEscalation: false, and drop all capabilities.
Configure these securityContext settings as defaults in Helm values and override subchart pods as needed.
Document pod security standards and settings in the README.
Test deployment to ensure all pods start successfully with the hardened securityContext.
Run a Kubernetes security scanner (e.g., kube-bench, kubesec) to verify compliance.
acceptance_criteria: All namespaces have PodSecurityAdmission labels enforcing restricted policy.
All pods (including test pods and Jobs) have secure securityContext settings.
Pods run as non-root, with read-only root filesystems and no privilege escalation.
README documents pod security standards.
Security scanner reports compliance with PSS.

title: Implement Security Event Logging and Audit Trail for Infrastructure
description: Enable and configure security event logging and audit trails for all infrastructure operations. This includes MinIO access/audit logs, CNPG Postgres authentication and DDL logs, Cilium network policy drop events, and alerting for security-relevant events. Ship all logs to a centralized logging system.
subtasks: Enable MinIO server access and audit logging to a dedicated log stream.
Configure CNPG Postgres to log connections, disconnections, and DDL statements.
Enable Cilium Hubble to export network policy drop events.
Update observability/alerting (task 1024) to include alerts for repeated auth failures, network policy denials, and Secret access anomalies.
Ship all security-relevant logs to a centralized logging system (e.g., ELK, Loki, or cloud-native alternative).
Document security logging configuration and alerting in the README.
Test by generating security events (e.g., failed logins, network denials) and verifying they are logged and alerts are triggered.
acceptance_criteria: MinIO, Postgres, and Cilium security events are logged and shipped centrally.
Alerts are configured for key security events.
README documents security logging and alerting setup.
Test events are visible in the logging system and trigger alerts.
